domain: Australian Aboriginal cognitive practice (songlines / dreaming tracks)
specific_mechanism: Songlines are sequence-indexed memory structures where knowledge is encoded as a path through a landscape: each location along the path carries a verse, story, and protocol; multiple distinct songlines can pass through the same physical location with different sequencing, different local interpretation, and different downstream successors; cross-community sharing is enabled by shared physical anchors with locally-specific interpretive protocols.
llm_application: Path-indexed (rather than node-indexed) agent memory: a memory architecture where retrieval keys are sequences-of-recent-nodes-along-a-traversal rather than single embedding similarities to the current query. The same conceptual node carries different downstream successors and different local interpretations conditional on which path the agent is currently on. Cross-task sharing happens at the path-segment level (when two tasks share a sub-sequence, learnings on the shared segment propagate), distinct from node-level vector similarity sharing.
novelty_claim: MemPalace (arxiv 2604.21284) applies method-of-loci spatial metaphor to LLM memory but uses node-indexed hierarchical storage (Wings → Rooms → Drawers), not path-indexed retrieval with shared-but-locally-reinterpreted nodes. Graph-based agent memory taxonomies (2602.05665) and trajectory-informed memory (2603.10600) operate at the node-and-edge level but do not formalize 'the same node has different successors and different interpretations conditional on which path is currently active' as a first-class structure. The Aboriginal songlines framing makes the path-as-key (rather than embedding-as-key) explicit.
